issues.body: The live base/default validation is performed only after `decide_budget`. Before reaching it, `budget["action"] == "needs-user"` calls `_pr_needs_user`, and `extend-model-pin` calls `worker-pr.py record-model-pin`. Thus a retargeted or unresolved-base needs-review PR is not actually removed from the loop before mutation: depending on its round posture, it can be relabeled/commented or have a durable pin recorded and then either exit or finally defer. The new test uses a pending top-tier fix posture whose budget action performs no mutation, so its `helper_calls == []` assertion cannot catch this ordering defect.
issues.fix_hint: Validate the live PR base ref against the protected default branch before comments/budget processing or any helper capable of mutating the PR. Add a regression posture that would otherwise take `needs-user` or `extend-model-pin`, retarget it, and assert that no helper is called and no allocator claim is attempted.
issues.title: Base admission check occurs after mutation-capable budget handling

verdict.summary: The new base-ref check is directionally correct and the added test proves that the selected pending-fix posture does not claim a reviewer. However, the check is placed after budget processing that can mutate the PR. A retargeted PR can therefore receive autonomous needs-user or model-pin mutations before it is excluded, contradicting the stated admission invariant and fail-closed/no-mutation intent. The regression test does not exercise either mutation-capable budget path.
